from the ninth to the twelfth, with Mars alone in the third.
The southern hemisphere has made him purely selfless to the
extent that he fought against odds defending his theories of
the infantile sexuality. He was occupied with concrete, visible,
and practical things. He became a pioneer in the inner side of
life in the depths of the unconscious. To add to all this, Mars
was in the third, and the third house governs the affairs of
everyday living. He has given us a method of adjusting our
selves to the changing environment.

The second chart is that of Napoleon, with planets in the
eastern part and with Jupiter isolated in the East. These
eastern hemisphere people select their own future and carry it
as they like. Just as Mars indicates a way of acting in which
the practical things are more important than ideal ones, so
